title: Let us play with rank list

content.problemStatement:
A **rank list** is a list of ranks of persons in a programming contest. Note that some of the persons might be having same rank. {1, 2}, {1, 2, 2} and {1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 4} are few examples of rank lists whereas {1, 3}, {0, 2}, {1, 2, 4} are not rank lists. 
Also note that a rank list need not to be sorted e.g. {2, 2, 1} and {3, 3, 2, 1} are valid rank lists.


Mathematically, a **rank list** is an array of numbers when sorted will have the starting element as 1 and difference between any two consecutive elements less than or equal to 1. 



A rank list is said to be an **ideal rank list** if no two persons gets equal rank in it. 

You can convert any rank list into an ideal rank list by applying following operations. In a single operation, you can change value of any one element of the rank list to any value. 

Chandan now wonders about minimum number of operations needed to convert a rank list of size **n** with sum of its element equal to **s** in to an ideal rank list. Please help Chandan find this minimum number of operations needed to create an ideal rank list.


Note that you are guaranteed that values of **n, s** will be given in such a way that there will exist a valid rank list.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers N and S, where N is the size of the rank list and S is the sum of its elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of operations needed to convert the rank list into an ideal rank list.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the given rank list is [1] which is already an ideal rank list, so we need 0 operations. For the second test case, the given rank list is [1, 2, 3] which is already an ideal rank list, so we need 0 operations. For the third test case, the given rank list is [1, 2, 2] which can be converted to an ideal rank list [1, 2, 3] by changing one element, so we need 1 operation. For the fourth test case, the given rank list is [1, 1, 1] which can be converted to an ideal rank list [1, 2, 3] by changing two elements, so we need 2 operations.

solution.approach: The key insight is to calculate the ideal sum of the rank list, which is the sum of the first N natural numbers. Then, we compare this ideal sum with the given sum S. If S is less than the ideal sum, we need to increase some elements in the rank list to make it an ideal rank list. The minimum number of operations needed is the difference between the ideal sum and S.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — no extra space is used.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n, s = map(int, input().split())
    ideal_sum = n * (n + 1) // 2
    print((ideal_sum - s) if s < ideal_sum else 0)
